headline: Half of Google Traffic Now Runs on IPv6
lede: The internet's next-generation protocol has officially reached a major milestone in global adoption. While the transition is far from uniform, dual-stack operations are now a standard reality for major service providers.

headline: A 3D Voxel Engine Built Entirely in APL
lede: An experimental project pushes APL's concise, symbolic notation into the demanding realm of 3D game architecture. The engine trades conventional C++ boilerplate for array-oriented logic to test the limits of the language.

headline: The Ongoing Failure to Understand CORS Security
lede: Developers continue to implement dangerous workarounds that bypass critical browser-based security policies. This neglect effectively leaves local networks vulnerable to third-party access and unauthorized data exfiltration.

headline: Optimizing Zigzag Decoding with AVX-512 Instructions
lede: Manual SIMD optimization offers significant throughput gains when compilers fall short. By leveraging specialized instructions like GFNI, developers can accelerate branchless arithmetic at the cost of strict hardware constraints.

headline: Revealing How iOS Apps Fingerprint Your Device
lede: A new research tool provides visibility into the metadata signals harvested by native mobile applications. It exposes how seemingly benign permissions facilitate the creation of unique, persistent device fingerprints.

headline: Finland Rethinks the Public Library as a Makerspace
lede: Public libraries in Finland have evolved into critical community hubs that provide access to tools beyond traditional books. This shift toward active civic infrastructure helps rebuild social trust and local engagement.

headline: Running MicroVMs Inside Proxmox for Rapid Isolation
lede: New packaging enables QEMU's microvm machine type to run within Proxmox with sub-300ms boot times. This approach offers a lightweight alternative to containers without sacrificing hardware-level KVM security.

headline: The Architectural Shift from Epoll to io_uring
lede: The Linux kernel is moving away from legacy poll-based notification systems toward batch-processed ring buffers. This transition promises to eliminate redundant context switches for high-concurrency workloads.
pullquote: The transition to io_uring marks the end of epoll as the standard for high-performance Linux network applications.

headline: How Controlled Breathing Modulates Neural Reward Encoding
lede: New neuroimaging data shows that slow, prolonged exhalation shifts cardiac parasympathetic activity to alter cognitive evaluation. This discovery bridges the gap between basic respiratory control and complex risk-taking behavior.

headline: Tracking the Evolution of Windows File Association
lede: From the functional dialogue boxes of the early nineties to the cloud-integrated services of today, Microsoft’s UX philosophy has shifted significantly. This historical review examines how these changes impacted the average user's interaction with the operating system.
